message.content:
Search the codebase thoroughly for any existing code in FormalConjectures/ and FormalConjecturesForMathlib/ that deals with:
1. SimpleGraph decomposition, cycle decomposition, edge partitioning
2. SimpleGraph.Walk, IsCycle usage patterns
3. Sym2, edgeFinset patterns
4. Any other Erdos problems that decompose graphs into cycles or edges

Report what you find, including file paths and line numbers. Be thorough - check both FormalConjectures/ and FormalConjecturesForMathlib/ directories.

    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 751
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/751](https://www.erdosproblems.com/751)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $G$ be a graph with chromatic number $\chi(G) = 4$. If $m_1 < m_2 < \cdots$ are the
    25‚Üílengths of the cycles in $G$, can $\min(m_{i+1} - m_i)$ be arbitrarily large?
    26‚Üí
    27‚ÜíThe answer is no: Bondy and Vince [BoVi98] proved that every graph with minimum degree
    28‚Üíat least $3$ has two cycles whose lengths differ by at most $2$, and hence the same is
    29‚Üítrue for every graph with chromatic number $4$.
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí[BoVi98] Bondy, J. A. and Vince, A., _Cycles in a graph whose lengths differ by one or
    32‚Üítwo_. Journal of Graph Theory (1998), 11-15.
    33‚Üí-/
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üínamespace Erdos751
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí/-- The set of cycle lengths occurring in a simple graph. -/
    40‚Üídef cycleLengths {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Set ‚Ñï :=
    41‚Üí  {n | ‚àÉ (v : V) (p : G.Walk v v), p.IsCycle ‚àß p.length = n}
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí/-- **Erd≈ës Problem 751**: If $G$ is a graph with $\chi(G) = 4$ and $m_1 < m_2 < \cdots$
    44‚Üíare the cycle lengths of $G$, the minimum gap $\min(m_{i+1} - m_i)$ cannot be made
    45‚Üíarbitrarily large. Bondy and Vince [BoVi98] proved that every graph with minimum degree
    46‚Üíat least $3$ has two cycles whose lengths differ by at most $2$, which implies the
    47‚Üínegative answer since $\chi(G) = 4$ forces a subgraph with minimum degree $\geq 3$.
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí[BoVi98] -/
    50‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    51‚Üítheorem erdos_751 : answer(False) ‚Üî
    52‚Üí    (‚àÄ C : ‚Ñï, ‚àÉ (V : Type) (_ : Fintype V) (G : SimpleGraph V),
    53‚Üí      G.chromaticNumber = 4 ‚àß
    54‚Üí        ‚àÄ m‚ÇÅ ‚àà cycleLengths G, ‚àÄ m‚ÇÇ ‚àà cycleLengths G,
    55‚Üí          m‚ÇÅ < m‚ÇÇ ‚Üí C ‚â§ m‚ÇÇ - m‚ÇÅ) := by

    58‚Üí/-- **Bondy‚ÄìVince Theorem**: Every graph with minimum degree at least $3$ contains two
    59‚Üícycles whose lengths differ by at most $2$. This is the stronger result that resolves
    60‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 751.

    62‚Üí[BoVi98] -/

    68‚Üí      m‚ÇÅ < m‚ÇÇ ‚àß m‚ÇÇ - m‚ÇÅ ‚â§ 2 := by

    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 65
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/65](https://www.erdosproblems.com/65)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíIs the sum of reciprocals of distinct cycle lengths in a graph on $n$ vertices with $kn$
    25‚Üíedges minimised by the complete bipartite graph? Gy√°rf√°s, Koml√≥s, and Szemer√©di [GKS84]
    26‚Üíproved $\sum 1/a_i \gg \log k$; Liu and Montgomery [LiMo20] proved the sharp bound.
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí[GKS84] Gy√°rf√°s, A., Koml√≥s, J., and Szemer√©di, E., *On the distribution of cycle lengths in
    29‚Üígraphs*, J. Graph Theory 8 (1984), 441‚Äì462.
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí[LiMo20] Liu, H. and Montgomery, R., *A solution to Erd≈ës and Hajnal's odd cycle problem*,
    32‚ÜíJ. Amer. Math. Soc. 36 (2023), 1191‚Äì1234.

    39‚Üí/-- The complete bipartite graph $K_{a,b}$ on `Fin (a + b)`, where vertices
    40‚Üí$\{0, \ldots, a-1\}$ form one part and $\{a, \ldots, a+b-1\}$ form the other. -/
    41‚Üídef completeBipartiteGraph65 (a b : ‚Ñï) : SimpleGraph (Fin (a + b)) where
    42‚Üí  Adj u v := (u.val < a ‚àß a ‚â§ v.val) ‚à® (a ‚â§ u.val ‚àß v.val < a)
    43‚Üí  symm u v h := by
    44‚Üí    rcases h with ‚ü®hu, hv‚ü© | ‚ü®hu, hv‚ü©
    45‚Üí    ¬∑ exact Or.inr ‚ü®hv, hu‚ü©
    46‚Üí    ¬∑ exact Or.inl ‚ü®hv, hu‚ü©
    47‚Üí  loopless v h := by rcases h with ‚ü®h1, h2‚ü© | ‚ü®h1, h2‚ü© <;> omega
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí/--
    50‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem #65 (Erd≈ës‚ÄìHajnal) [GKS84] [LiMo20]:
    51‚ÜíLet $G$ be a graph with $n$ vertices and $kn$ edges, and let $a_1 < a_2 < \cdots$ be the
    52‚Üídistinct lengths of cycles in $G$. Is it true that $\sum 1/a_i \gg \log k$? Is the sum
    53‚Üí$\sum 1/a_i$ minimised when $G$ is a complete bipartite graph?
    54‚Üí
    55‚ÜíThe first question was proved by Gy√°rf√°s, Koml√≥s, and Szemer√©di [GKS84].
    56‚ÜíLiu and Montgomery [LiMo20] proved the asymptotically sharp lower bound
    57‚Üí$\geq (1/2 - o(1)) \log k$.
    58‚Üí
    59‚ÜíThe remaining open question is formalized below: for any graph $G$ on $n$ vertices whose edge
    60‚Üícount equals $a \cdot b$ for some partition $a + b = n$, the sum of reciprocals of distinct cycle
    61‚Üílengths of $G$ is at least the corresponding sum for the complete bipartite graph $K_{a,b}$.
    62‚Üí-/
    63‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]

    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíThere exists a density-zero set $A$ and a constant $c > 0$ such that every graph with
    25‚Üíaverage degree at least $c$ contains a cycle whose length is in $A$. Proved by
    26‚ÜíVerstra√´te [Ve05]; Liu and Montgomery [LiMo20] showed powers of $2$ work.

    41‚Üísufficiently large $n$, $|A \cap \{0, \ldots, n\}| \leq \varepsilon \cdot n$.

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 810
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/810](https://www.erdosproblems.com/810)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíA problem of Burr, Erd≈ës, Graham, and S√≥s. Asks whether there exists Œµ > 0 such that, for all
    25‚Üísufficiently large n, some n-vertex graph with at least Œµn¬≤ edges admits an n-coloring of its edges
    26‚Üíwhere every C‚ÇÑ is rainbow.
    27‚Üí-/
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üínamespace Erdos810
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí/-- A graph on $n$ vertices admits a rainbow $C_4$ coloring with $n$ colors if there
    32‚Üí    exists an edge coloring using $n$ colors such that every 4-cycle in the
    33‚Üí    graph has all 4 edges receiving distinct colors. -/
    34‚Üídef AdmitsRainbowC4Coloring (n : ‚Ñï) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) : Prop :=
    35‚Üí  ‚àÉ c : Sym2 (Fin n) ‚Üí Fin n,
    36‚Üí    ‚àÄ (a b d e : Fin n),
    37‚Üí      a ‚âÝ b ‚Üí a ‚âÝ d ‚Üí a ‚âÝ e ‚Üí b ‚âÝ d ‚Üí b ‚âÝ e ‚Üí d ‚âÝ e ‚Üí
    38‚Üí      G.Adj a b ‚Üí G.Adj b d ‚Üí G.Adj d e ‚Üí G.Adj e a ‚Üí
    39‚Üí      c (Sym2.mk (a, b)) ‚âÝ c (Sym2.mk (b, d)) ‚àß
    40‚Üí      c (Sym2.mk (a, b)) ‚âÝ c (Sym2.mk (d, e)) ‚àß
    41‚Üí      c (Sym2.mk (a, b)) ‚âÝ c (Sym2.mk (e, a)) ‚àß
    42‚Üí      c (Sym2.mk (b, d)) ‚âÝ c (Sym2.mk (d, e)) ‚àß
    43‚Üí      c (Sym2.mk (b, d)) ‚âÝ c (Sym2.mk (e, a)) ‚àß
    44‚Üí      c (Sym2.mk (d, e)) ‚âÝ c (Sym2.mk (e, a))
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí/--
    47‚Üí**Erd≈ës Problem 810**: Does there exist some $\varepsilon > 0$ such that, for all
    48‚Üísufficiently large $n$, there exists a graph $G$ on $n$ vertices with at least
    49‚Üí$\varepsilon n^2$ many edges such that the edges can be coloured with $n$ colours so that
    50‚Üíevery $C_4$ receives 4 distinct colours?
    51‚Üí-/
    52‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    53‚Üítheorem erdos_810 : answer(sorry) ‚Üî
    54‚Üí    ‚àÉ Œµ : ‚Ñù, 0 < Œµ ‚àß
    55‚Üí    ‚àÉ N : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• N ‚Üí
    56‚Üí      ‚àÉ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),

    58‚Üí        AdmitsRainbowC4Coloring n G := by

    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 811
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/811](https://www.erdosproblems.com/811)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíSuppose $n \equiv 1 \pmod{m}$. We say that an edge-colouring of $K_n$ using $m$ colours
    25‚Üíis balanced if every vertex sees exactly $\lfloor n/m \rfloor$ edges of each colour.
    26‚Üí
    27‚ÜíFor which graphs $G$ is it true that, if $m = e(G)$, for all large $n \equiv 1 \pmod{m}$,
    28‚Üíevery balanced edge-colouring of $K_n$ with $m$ colours contains a rainbow copy
    29‚Üíof $G$? (That is, a subgraph isomorphic to $G$ where each edge receives a
    30‚Üídifferent colour.)
    31‚Üí
    32‚ÜíA problem of Erd≈ës, Pyber, and Tuza.
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí[Er91] Erd≈ës, P., _Some of my favourite problems in various branches of combinatorics_, 1991.
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí[ErTu93] Erd≈ës, P. and Tuza, Zs., _Rainbow subgraphs in edge-colorings of complete graphs_, 1993.
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí[Er96] Erd≈ës, P., _Some recent problems and results in graph theory_, 1996.
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí[ClWa23] Clemen, F. and Wagner, A., 2023.
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí[AxCl24] Axenovich, M. and Clemen, F., 2024.
    43‚Üí-/
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üíopen SimpleGraph Finset
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üínamespace Erdos811
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí/-- The cycle graph on $m$ vertices (for $m \ge 3$): vertex $i$ is adjacent
    50‚Üí    to vertices $(i+1) \bmod m$ and $(i-1) \bmod m$. -/
    51‚Üídef cycleGraph811 (m : ‚Ñï) (_ : m ‚â• 3) : SimpleGraph (Fin m) where
    52‚Üí  Adj i j := i ‚âÝ j ‚àß (j.val = (i.val + 1) % m ‚à® i.val = (j.val + 1) % m)
    53‚Üí  symm := fun _ _ ‚ü®hne, h‚ü© => ‚ü®hne.symm, h.elim Or.inr Or.inl‚ü©
    54‚Üí  loopless := fun _ ‚ü®h, _‚ü© => h rfl
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/-- An edge-colouring of the complete graph on $\operatorname{Fin} n$ with $m$ colours is
    57‚Üí    **balanced** if every vertex sees exactly $\lfloor n / m \rfloor$ edges of each colour.
    58‚Üí    (When $n \equiv 1 \pmod{m}$, the degree $n - 1$ divides evenly as
    59‚Üí    $m \cdot \lfloor n / m \rfloor$.) -/
    60‚Üídef IsBalancedColoring (n m : ‚Ñï) (c : Sym2 (Fin n) ‚Üí Fin m) : Prop :=
    61‚Üí  ‚àÄ (v : Fin n) (i : Fin m),
    62‚Üí    (Finset.univ.filter (fun w : Fin n => w ‚âÝ v ‚àß
    63‚Üí      c (Sym2.mk (v, w)) = i)).card = n / m
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí/-- A **rainbow copy** of a graph $G$ in a coloured complete graph on $\operatorname{Fin} n$
    66‚Üí    is an injective vertex map such that all edges of $G$ receive pairwise
    67‚Üí    distinct colours. -/
    68‚Üídef HasRainbowCopy {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V)
    69‚Üí    {n m : ‚Ñï} (c : Sym2 (Fin n) ‚Üí Fin m) : Prop :=
    70‚Üí  ‚àÉ f : V ‚Üí Fin n, Function.Injective f ‚àß
    71‚Üí    ‚àÄ a b a' b' : V, G.Adj a b ‚Üí G.Adj a' b' ‚Üí
    72‚Üí      Sym2.mk (a, b) ‚âÝ Sym2.mk (a', b') ‚Üí
    73‚Üí      c (Sym2.mk (f a, f b)) ‚âÝ c (Sym2.mk (f a', f b'))
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üí/--
    76‚Üí**Erd≈ës Problem 811** (specific challenge from [Er91] and [Er96]):
    77‚Üí
    78‚ÜíFor all sufficiently large $n \equiv 1 \pmod{6}$, every balanced edge-colouring
    79‚Üíof $K_n$ with $6$ colours contains a rainbow copy of $C_6$.
    80‚Üí
    81‚ÜíThe companion question about $K_4$ (which also has $6$ edges) was answered
    82‚Üínegatively by Clemen and Wagner [ClWa23].
    83‚Üí-/
    84‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    85‚Üítheorem erdos_811 :
    86‚Üí    ‚àÉ N : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• N ‚Üí n % 6 = 1 ‚Üí
    87‚Üí      ‚àÄ c : Sym2 (Fin n) ‚Üí Fin 6,
    88‚Üí        IsBalancedColoring n 6 c ‚Üí
    89‚Üí        HasRainbowCopy (cycleGraph811 6 (by omega)) c := by
    90‚Üí  sorry
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üíend Erdos811
    93‚Üí

    24‚Üí
    25‚Üíopen Classical Finset List
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üívariable {Œ± : Type*} [Fintype Œ±] [DecidableEq Œ±]
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí/-- `Ls G` is the maximum number of leaves over all spanning trees of `G`.
    30‚ÜíIt is defined to be `0` when `G` is not connected. -/
    31‚Üínoncomputable def Ls (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñù :=
    32‚Üí  letI spanningTrees := { T : Subgraph G | T.IsSpanning ‚àß IsTree T.coe }
    33‚Üí  letI leaves (T : Subgraph G) := T.verts.toFinset.filter (fun v => T.degree v = 1)
    34‚Üí  letI num_leaves (T : Subgraph G) := (leaves T).card
    35‚Üí  sSup (Set.image (fun T => (num_leaves T : ‚Ñù)) spanningTrees)
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/-- `n G` is the number of vertices of `G` as a real number. -/
    38‚Üínoncomputable def n (_ : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñù := Fintype.card Œ±
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí/-- `m G` is the size of a maximum matching of `G`. -/
    41‚Üínoncomputable def m (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñù :=
    42‚Üí  let matchings := { M : Subgraph G | M.IsMatching }
    43‚Üí  sSup (Set.image (fun M => (M.edgeSet.toFinset.card : ‚Ñù)) matchings)
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/-- The maximum cardinality among all independent sets `s`
    46‚Üí    that maximize the quantity `|s| - |N(s)|`, where `N(s)`
    47‚Üí    is the neighborhood of the set `s`. -/
    48‚Üínoncomputable def aprime (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñù :=
    49‚Üí  letI indep_sets : Finset (Finset Œ±) := univ.powerset.filter (fun s => G.IsIndepSet (s : Set Œ±))
    50‚Üí  letI diff (s : Finset Œ±) : ‚Ñ§ := (s.card : ‚Ñ§) - (‚ãÉ v ‚àà (s : Set Œ±), G.neighborSet v).toFinset.card
    51‚Üí  letI max_diff := (indep_sets.image diff).max
    52‚Üí  letI critical_sets := indep_sets.filter (fun s ‚Ü¶ diff s = max_diff.getD 0)
    53‚Üí  letI max_card := (critical_sets.image Finset.card).max
    54‚Üí  (max_card.getD 0 : ‚Ñù)
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/-- `largestInducedForestSize G` is the size of a largest induced forest of `G`. -/
    57‚Üínoncomputable def largestInducedForestSize (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
    58‚Üí  sSup { n | ‚àÉ s : Finset Œ±, (G.induce s).IsAcyclic ‚àß s.card = n }
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí/-- The degree sequence of a graph, sorted in nondecreasing order. -/
    61‚Üínoncomputable def degreeSequence (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : List ‚Ñï :=
    62‚Üí  (Finset.univ.val.map fun v : Œ± => G.degree v).sort (¬∑ ‚â§ ¬∑)
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí/--
    65‚ÜíThe maximum number of occurrences of any term of the degree sequence of `G`.
    66‚Üí-/
    67‚Üínoncomputable def degreeSequenceMultiplicity (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñï :=
    68‚Üí  letI degs := degreeSequence G
    69‚Üí  (List.max? (degs.map (fun d => degs.count d))).getD 0
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üí/-- `largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G` is the size of a largest induced
    72‚Üíbipartite subgraph of `G`. -/
    73‚Üínoncomputable def largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
    74‚Üí  sSup { n | ‚àÉ s : Finset Œ±, (G.induce s).IsBipartite ‚àß s.card = n }
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí/-- `b G` is the number of vertices of a largest induced bipartite subgraph of `G`.
    77‚ÜíReturned as a real number. -/
    78‚Üínoncomputable def b (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñù :=
    79‚Üí  (largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G : ‚Ñù)
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí/-- Independence number of the neighbourhood of `v`. -/
    82‚Üínoncomputable def indepNeighborsCard (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) (v : Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
    83‚Üí  (G.induce (G.neighborSet v)).indepNum
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí/-- The same quantity as a real number. -/
    86‚Üínoncomputable def indepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) (v : Œ±) : ‚Ñù :=
    87‚Üí  (indepNeighborsCard G v : ‚Ñù)
    88‚Üí
    89‚Üí/-- Average of `indepNeighbors` over all vertices. -/
    90‚Üínoncomputable def averageIndepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñù :=
    91‚Üí  (‚àë v ‚àà Finset.univ, indepNeighbors G v) / (Fintype.card Œ± : ‚Ñù)
    92‚Üí
    93‚Üí/-- A unit distance graph in ‚Ñù¬≤:
    94‚ÜíA graph where the vertices V are a collection of points in ‚Ñù¬≤ and there is
    95‚Üían edge between two points if and only if the distance between them is 1. -/
    96‚Üídef UnitDistancePlaneGraph (V : Set (EuclideanSpace ‚Ñù (Fin 2))) : SimpleGraph V where
    97‚Üí  Adj x y := Dist.dist x y = 1
    98‚Üí  symm x y := by simp [PseudoMetricSpace.dist_comm]
    99‚Üí
   100‚Üí/--
   101‚ÜíTwo walks are internally disjoint if they share no vertices other than their endpoints.
   102‚Üí-/
   103‚Üídef InternallyDisjoint {V : Type*} {G : SimpleGraph V} {u v x y : V}
   104‚Üí    (p : G.Walk u v) (q : G.Walk x y) : Prop :=
   105‚Üí  Disjoint p.support.tail.dropLast q.support.tail.dropLast
   106‚Üí
   107‚Üí/--
   108‚ÜíWe say a graph is infinitely connected if any two vertices are connected by infinitely many
   109‚Üípairwise disjoint paths.
   110‚Üí-/
   111‚Üídef InfinitelyConnected {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Prop :=
   112‚Üí  Pairwise fun u v ‚Ü¶ ‚àÉ P : Set (G.Walk u v),
   113‚Üí    P.Infinite ‚àß (‚àÄ p ‚àà P, p.IsPath) ‚àß P.Pairwise InternallyDisjoint
   114‚Üí
   115‚Üí/-- Infinite graphs: definitions for max degree and clique number so that the maximum
   116‚Üídegree (resp. clique number) of a graph with unbounded degree (resp. clique size) is
   117‚Üí`‚àû` rather than 0.
   118‚Üí-/
   119‚Üínoncomputable
   120‚Üídef edegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (v : V) : ‚Ñï‚àû := (G.neighborSet v).encard
   121‚Üí
   122‚Üínoncomputable
   123‚Üídef emaxDegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ñï‚àû := ‚®Ü v, G.edegree v
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üínoncomputable
   126‚Üídef ecliqueNum {V : Type} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ñï‚àû := ‚®Ü (s : Finset V) (_ : G.IsClique s), #s
   127‚Üí
   128‚Üíend SimpleGraph

    31‚Üívariable {Œ± : Type*} [Fintype Œ±] [DecidableEq Œ±]
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí/-- Abbreviation for the average independence number of the neighborhoods.
    34‚Üí-/
    35‚Üínoncomputable abbrev l (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñù := averageIndepNeighbors G
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/-- The same quantity under a different name, used in some conjectures.
    38‚Üí-/
    39‚Üínoncomputable abbrev l_avg (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñù := averageIndepNeighbors G
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí/-- Independent domination number of `G`. -/
    42‚Üínoncomputable def gi (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñï := G.indepDominationNumber
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- `temp_v G v = deg(v)/(n(G) - deg(v))`. -/
    45‚Üínoncomputable def temp_v (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] (v : Œ±) : ‚Ñù :=
    46‚Üí  let n := Fintype.card Œ±
    47‚Üí  let deg := G.degree v
    48‚Üí  if n = deg then 0 else (deg : ‚Ñù) / ((n : ‚Ñù) - (deg : ‚Ñù))
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí/-- Maximum of `temp_v` over all vertices. -/
    51‚Üínoncomputable def MaxTemp (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] [Fintype Œ±] [Nonempty Œ±] : ‚Ñù :=
    52‚Üí  let temps := Finset.univ.image (temp_v G)
    53‚Üí  temps.max' (Finset.image_nonempty.mpr Finset.univ_nonempty)
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí/-- Cardinality of the union of the neighbourhoods of the ends of the non-edge `e`. -/
    56‚Üídef non_edge_neighborhood_card (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] (e : Sym2 Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
    57‚Üí  Sym2.lift ‚ü®fun u v => (G.neighborFinset u ‚à™ G.neighborFinset v).card,
    58‚Üí    fun u v => by simp [Finset.union_comm]‚ü© e
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí/-- Minimum size of the neighbourhood of a non-edge of `G`. -/
    61‚Üínoncomputable def NG (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñù :=
    62‚Üí  let non_edges := (compl G).edgeFinset
    63‚Üí  if h : non_edges.Nonempty then
    64‚Üí    let neighbor_sizes := non_edges.image (non_edge_neighborhood_card G)
    65‚Üí    (neighbor_sizes.min' (Finset.Nonempty.image h _))
    66‚Üí  else
    67‚Üí    (Fintype.card Œ± : ‚Ñù)
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí/-- Size of a largest induced forest of `G` expressed as a real number. -/
    70‚Üínoncomputable def S (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñù :=
    71‚Üí  let card := Fintype.card Œ±
    72‚Üí  if card < 2 then 0 else
    73‚Üí    let degrees := Multiset.ofList (List.map (fun v => G.degree v) Finset.univ.toList)
    74‚Üí    let sorted_degrees := degrees.sort (¬∑ ‚â§ ¬∑)
    75‚Üí    ‚Üë((sorted_degrees[card - 2]?).getD 0)
    76‚Üí
    77‚Üí/-- Local eccentricity of a vertex. -/
    78‚Üínoncomputable def local_eccentricity (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) (v : Œ±) : ENat :=
    79‚Üí  sSup (Set.range (G.edist v))
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí/-- The largest integer less than or equal to `x`. -/
    82‚Üínoncomputable def FLOOR (x : ‚Ñù) : ‚Ñù := ‚åäx‚åã
    83‚Üí
    84‚Üí/-- Eccentricity of a vertex. -/
    85‚Üínoncomputable def eccentricity (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) (v : Œ±) : ‚Ñï‚àû :=
    86‚Üí  sSup (Set.range (G.edist v))
    87‚Üí
    88‚Üí/-- The minimum eccentricity among all vertices. -/
    89‚Üínoncomputable def minEccentricity (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñï‚àû :=
    90‚Üí  sInf (Set.range (eccentricity G))
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üí/-- The set of vertices of minimum eccentricity. -/
    93‚Üínoncomputable def graphCenter (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : Set Œ± :=
    94‚Üí  {v : Œ± | eccentricity G v = minEccentricity G}
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí/-- The maximum eccentricity among all vertices. -/
    97‚Üínoncomputable def maxEccentricity (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñï‚àû :=
    98‚Üí  sSup (Set.range (eccentricity G))
    99‚Üí
   100‚Üí/-- The set of vertices of maximum eccentricity. -/
   101‚Üínoncomputable def maxEccentricityVertices (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : Set Œ± :=
   102‚Üí  {v : Œ± | eccentricity G v = maxEccentricity G}
   103‚Üí
   104‚Üí/-- Distance from a vertex to a finite set. -/
   105‚Üínoncomputable def distToSet (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) (v : Œ±) (S : Set Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
   106‚Üí  if h : S.toFinset.Nonempty then
   107‚Üí    (S.toFinset.image (fun s => G.dist v s)).min' (Finset.Nonempty.image h _)
   108‚Üí  else 0
   109‚Üí
   110‚Üí/-- Average distance of `G`. -/
   111‚Üínoncomputable def averageDistance (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñù :=
   112‚Üí  if Fintype.card Œ± > 1 then
   113‚Üí    (‚àë u ‚àà Finset.univ, ‚àë v ‚àà Finset.univ, (G.dist u v : ‚Ñù)) /
   114‚Üí      ((Fintype.card Œ± : ‚Ñù) * ((Fintype.card Œ± : ‚Ñù) - 1))
   115‚Üí  else
   116‚Üí    0
   117‚Üí
   118‚Üí/-- The floor of the average distance of `G`. -/
   119‚Üínoncomputable def path (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
   120‚Üí  if G.Connected then
   121‚Üí    Nat.floor (averageDistance G)
   122‚Üí  else
   123‚Üí    0
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üí/-- Auxiliary quantity `ecc` used in conjecture 34. -/
   126‚Üínoncomputable def ecc (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) (S : Set Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
   127‚Üí  let s_comp := Finset.univ.filter (fun v => v ‚àâ S)
   128‚Üí  if h : s_comp.Nonempty then
   129‚Üí    (s_comp.image (fun v => distToSet G v S)).max' (Finset.Nonempty.image h _)
   130‚Üí  else 0
   131‚Üí
   132‚Üí/-- Average distance from all vertices to a given set. -/
   133‚Üínoncomputable def distavg (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) (S : Set Œ±) : ‚Ñù :=
   134‚Üí  if Fintype.card Œ± > 0 then
   135‚Üí    (‚àë v ‚àà Finset.univ, (distToSet G v S : ‚Ñù)) / (Fintype.card Œ± : ‚Ñù)
   136‚Üí  else
   137‚Üí    0
   138‚Üí
   139‚Üí/-- A family of paths covering all vertices without overlaps. -/
   140‚Üídef IsPathCover (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) (P : Finset (Finset Œ±)) : Prop :=
   141‚Üí  (‚àÄ s1 ‚àà P, ‚àÄ s2 ‚àà P, s1 ‚âÝ s2 ‚Üí Disjoint s1 s2) ‚àß
   142‚Üí  (Finset.univ ‚äÜ P.biUnion id) ‚àß
   143‚Üí  (‚àÄ s ‚àà P, ‚àÉ (u v : Œ±) (p : G.Walk u v), p.IsPath ‚àß s = p.support.toFinset)
   144‚Üí
   145‚Üí/-- Minimum size of a path cover of `G`. -/
   146‚Üínoncomputable def pathCoverNumber (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
   147‚Üí  sInf { k | ‚àÉ P : Finset (Finset Œ±), P.card = k ‚àß IsPathCover G P }
   148‚Üí
   149‚Üí/-- The same quantity as a real number. -/
   150‚Üínoncomputable def p (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñù := (pathCoverNumber G : ‚Ñù)
   151‚Üí
   152‚Üí/-- The Wiener index of `G`, which is the sum of distances between all
   153‚Üípairs of vertices. -/
   154‚Üínoncomputable def wienerIndex (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
   155‚Üí  ‚àë uv : Sym2 Œ±, uv.lift ‚ü®fun u v ‚Ü¶ G.dist u v, by simp [dist_comm]‚ü©
   156‚Üí
   157‚Üí/-- Auxiliary function for Szeged index: counts vertices closer to u than v. -/
   158‚Üínoncomputable def szeged_aux (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) (u v : Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
   159‚Üí  (Finset.univ.filter (fun w => G.edist w u < G.edist w v)).card
   160‚Üí
   161‚Üí/-- The Szeged index of `G`.
   162‚Üí
   163‚ÜíThis is define as the sum `‚àë_{uv ‚àà E(G)} n_u(u,v) * n_v(u,v)` where
   164‚Üí`n_u(uv)` is the number of vertices closer to `u` than `v`.
   165‚Üí-/
   166‚Üínoncomputable def szegedIndex (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñï :=
   167‚Üí  ‚àë e ‚àà G.edgeFinset,
   168‚Üí    e.lift ‚ü®fun u v => szeged_aux G u v * szeged_aux G v u, by simp [mul_comm]‚ü©
   169‚Üí
   170‚Üí/-- The average degree of `G`. -/
   171‚Üínoncomputable def averageDegree (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñö  :=
   172‚Üí  (‚àë v, (G.degree v : ‚Ñö)) / (Fintype.card Œ± : ‚Ñö)
   173‚Üí
   174‚Üí/-- The multiset of degrees of a graph. -/
   175‚Üídef degreeMultiset (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : Multiset ‚Ñï :=
   176‚Üí  Finset.univ.val.map fun v => G.degree v
   177‚Üí
   178‚Üí/-- The annihilation number of a graph. This is the largest number of degrees that can be added
   179‚Üítogether without going over the total number of edges of that graph. -/
   180‚Üídef annihilationNumber (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñï :=
   181‚Üí  -- Calculate the limit: The number of edges (Sum of degrees / 2)
   182‚Üí  letI limit := G.edgeFinset.card
   183‚Üí
   184‚Üí  -- The set of all multisets of degrees that sum to less than or equal to `limit`
   185‚Üí  Finset.Iic (degreeMultiset G)
   186‚Üí    |>.filter (fun S ‚Ü¶ Multiset.sum S ‚â§ limit)
   187‚Üí    |>.sup Multiset.card
   188‚Üí
   189‚Üí/--
   190‚ÜíComputes the annihilation number of a graph G.
   191‚Üí
   192‚ÜíIt calculates the degree sequence, sorts it ascendingly, and finds the largest prefix length 'k'
   193‚Üí(where `0 ‚â§ k ‚â§ |V(G)|`) such that the sum of the prefix is less than or equal to the sum of the corresponding suffix.
   194‚Üí-/
   195‚Üínoncomputable def annihilationNumber' (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñï :=
   196‚Üí  -- 1. Get the degree sequence sorted in ascending order.
   197‚Üí  -- G.degree_list returns the list of degrees.
   198‚Üí  letI degrees := (Finset.univ.image fun v => G.degree v).sort (¬∑ ‚â§ ¬∑)
   199‚Üí
   200‚Üí  -- 2. Define the condition for the annihilation number.
   201‚Üí  -- k represents the number of smallest degrees considered (the length of the prefix).
   202‚Üí  letI condition (k : ‚Ñï) : Bool := (degrees.take k).sum ‚â§ (degrees.drop k).sum
   203‚Üí
   204‚Üí  -- 3. Find the maximum k in {0, ..., n} satisfying the condition.
   205‚Üí  -- List.range (n + 1) generates the list [0, 1, ..., n].
   206‚Üí  letI candidates := (List.range (Fintype.card Œ± + 1)).filter condition
   207‚Üí
   208‚Üí  -- 4. Return the maximum candidate.
   209‚Üí  -- The list of candidates is guaranteed to be non-empty because k=0 always satisfies
   210‚Üí  -- the condition (0 ‚â§ sum of all degrees).
   211‚Üí  candidates.getLast!
   212‚Üí
   213‚Üíset_option linter.unusedSectionVars false in
   214‚Üí-- TODO(Paul-Lez): debug the issue with the unused variable linter...
   215‚Üíproof_wanted annihilationNumberEq (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] :
   216‚Üí    annihilationNumber G = annihilationNumber' G

   218‚Üí/-!
   219‚Üí## Residue
   220‚ÜíThe residue of a graph is the number of zeros remaining after iteratively applying the Havel-Hakimi
   221‚Üíalgorithm to the degree sequence until all remaining degrees are zero.
   222‚Üí-/
   223‚Üí
   224‚Üí/--
   225‚ÜíHelper function: Performs one step of the Havel-Hakimi reduction on a degree sequence.
   226‚ÜíAssumes the input list `s` is sorted descending.
   227‚ÜíRemoves the first element `d`, decrements the next `d` elements by 1, and re-sorts the list descending.
   228‚Üí
   229‚ÜíNote: when `s` is the list of vertices arising from a simple graph, if the first index is `s` then
   230‚Üíthe degree list always has length at least `s+1` so this makes sense.
   231‚Üí-/
   232‚Üíprivate def havelHakimiStep (s : List ‚Ñï) : List ‚Ñï :=
   233‚Üí  match s with
   234‚Üí  | [] => []
   235‚Üí  | d :: rest =>
   236‚Üí    -- Split the rest into the part to decrement (first d elements) and the remaining part.
   237‚Üí    let (to_decrement, remaining) := rest.splitAt d
   238‚Üí    -- Decrement the elements
   239‚Üí    let decremented := to_decrement.map (¬∑ - 1)
   240‚Üí    -- Combine and re-sort descending.
   241‚Üí    (decremented ++ remaining).mergeSort (¬∑ ‚â• ¬∑)
   242‚Üí
   243‚Üí/--
   244‚ÜíAuxiliary function to calculate the residue recursively.
   245‚ÜíApplies Havel-Hakimi steps until the sequence consists only of zeros or is empty.
   246‚Üí-/
   247‚Üíprivate partial def residueAux : List ‚Ñï ‚Üí ‚Ñï
   248‚Üí  | [] => 0        -- Empty sequence, residue is 0.
   249‚Üí  | 0 :: s => 1 + s.length -- If the largest degree is 0 (and the list is sorted), all are 0.
   250‚Üí  | s => residueAux (havelHakimiStep s) -- Apply one reduction step and recurse.
   251‚Üí
   252‚Üí/--
   253‚ÜíComputes the residue of a graph G, ,i.e. the number of zeros remaining after iteratively applying the Havel-Hakimi
   254‚Üíalgorithm to the degree sequence until all remaining degrees are zero.
   255‚ÜíStarts with the descending degree sequence and applies the Havel-Hakimi process.
   256‚Üí-/
   257‚Üínoncomputable def residue (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñï :=
   258‚Üí  -- Get the degree sequence sorted in descending order and apply `residueAux`.
   259‚Üí  residueAux ((Finset.univ.image fun v => G.degree v).sort (¬∑ ‚â• ¬∑))
   260‚Üí
   261‚Üí/--
   262‚ÜíFractional alpha. This is defined as
   263‚Üí$$
   264‚Üí\max_x \sum_{v \in V} x_v
   265‚Üí$$
   266‚Üísubject to $0 \le x_v \le 1$ for all $v \in V$ and
   267‚Üí$x_u + x_v \le 1$ whenever $(u, v)$ are connected by an edge.
   268‚Üí-/
   269‚Üínoncomputable def fractionalAlpha (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñù :=
   270‚Üí  sSup {(‚àë i, x i) | (x : Œ± ‚Üí ‚Ñù) (_ : ‚àÄ v, x v ‚àà Set.Icc 0 1)
   271‚Üí    (_ : ‚àÄ u v, G.Adj u v ‚Üí x u + x v ‚â§ 1)}
   272‚Üí
   273‚Üí/--
   274‚ÜíLov√°sz Theta Function (œë(G))
   275‚ÜíThe Lov√°sz theta function is defined as:
   276‚Üíœë(G) = min Œª_max(A)
   277‚Üíwhere the minimum is taken over all symmetric matrices A such that:
   278‚Üí
   279‚ÜíA_ij = 1 for all i = j (diagonal entries are 1)
   280‚ÜíA_ij = 0 for all {i,j} ‚àà E (entries corresponding to edges are 0)
   281‚ÜíA is positive semidefinite
   282‚Üí
   283‚ÜíHere Œª_max(A) denotes the maximum eigenvalue of A.
   284‚Üí-/
   285‚Üínoncomputable def lovaczThetaFunction
   286‚Üí    (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñù :=
   287‚Üí  sInf {(Matrix.IsHermitian.maxEigenvalue hA) | (A : Matrix Œ± Œ± ‚Ñù) (hA : A.IsHermitian)
   288‚Üí      (_ : ‚àÄ i, A i i = 1) (_ : ‚àÄ i j, G.Adj i j ‚Üí A i j = 0)}
   289‚Üí
   290‚Üí/--
   291‚ÜíGiven a simple graph `G` with adjacency matrix `A`, this is the number `n‚ÇÄ + min n‚Çä n‚Çã` where:
   292‚Üí- `n‚ÇÄ` is the multiplicity of zero as an eigenvalue of `A`
   293‚Üí- `n‚Çä` is the number of positive eigenvalues of `A` (counting multiplicities)
   294‚Üí- `n‚Çã` is the number of negative eigenvalues of `A` (counting multiplicities)
   295‚Üí-/
   296‚Üínoncomputable def cvetkovic (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñï :=
   297‚Üí  letI A : Matrix Œ± Œ± ‚Ñù := G.adjMatrix ‚Ñù
   298‚Üí  letI spectrum : Multiset ‚Ñù := (Matrix.charpoly A).roots
   299‚Üí  letI positive_count : ‚Ñï := spectrum.countP (fun x => 0 < x)
   300‚Üí  letI negative_count : ‚Ñï := spectrum.countP (fun x => 0 > x)
   301‚Üí  letI zero_count : ‚Ñï := spectrum.countP (fun x => 0 = x)
   302‚Üí  zero_count + min positive_count negative_count
   303‚Üí
   304‚Üíend SimpleGraph
   305‚Üí

    13‚ÜíSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14‚Üílimitations under the License.

    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 585
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/585](https://www.erdosproblems.com/585)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíWhat is the maximum number of edges that a graph on $n$ vertices can have if it
    25‚Üídoes not contain two edge-disjoint cycles with the same vertex set?
    26‚Üí
    27‚ÜíA problem of Erd≈ës [Er76b].
    28‚Üí
    29‚ÜíPyber, R√∂dl, and Szemer√©di [PRS95] constructed such a graph with
    30‚Üí$\gg n \log \log n$ edges (lower bound).
    31‚Üí
    32‚ÜíChakraborti, Janzer, Methuku, and Montgomery [CJMM24] showed that such a
    33‚Üígraph can have at most $n \cdot (\log n)^{O(1)}$ many edges (upper bound).
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí[Er76b] Erd≈ës, P., _Problems in combinatorial and graph theory_ (1976).
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí[PRS95] Pyber, L., R√∂dl, V., and Szemer√©di, E., _Dense graphs without
    38‚Üí3-regular subgraphs_ (1995).
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí[CJMM24] Chakraborti, D., Janzer, O., Methuku, A., and Montgomery, R. (2024).
    41‚Üí-/
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üíopen SimpleGraph Classical
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üínamespace Erdos585
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí/-- A graph has no two edge-disjoint cycles with the same vertex set. -/
    48‚Üídef NoTwoEdgeDisjointCyclesSameVertexSet {n : ‚Ñï} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) : Prop :=
    49‚Üí  ¬¨‚àÉ (u v : Fin n) (c‚ÇÅ : G.Walk u u) (c‚ÇÇ : G.Walk v v),
    50‚Üí    c‚ÇÅ.IsCycle ‚àß c‚ÇÇ.IsCycle ‚àß
    51‚Üí    c‚ÇÅ.support.toFinset = c‚ÇÇ.support.toFinset ‚àß
    52‚Üí    Disjoint c‚ÇÅ.edges.toFinset c‚ÇÇ.edges.toFinset
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí/-- The maximum number of edges in a graph on $n$ vertices with no two
    55‚Üíedge-disjoint cycles sharing the same vertex set. -/
    56‚Üínoncomputable def maxEdgesNoEdgeDisjointCycles (n : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï :=
    57‚Üí  sSup {m : ‚Ñï | ‚àÉ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
    58‚Üí    NoTwoEdgeDisjointCyclesSameVertexSet G ‚àß G.edgeFinset.card = m}
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí/--
    61‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 585:
    62‚Üí
    63‚ÜíWhat is the maximum number of edges that a graph on $n$ vertices can have if it
    64‚Üídoes not contain two edge-disjoint cycles with the same vertex set?
    65‚Üí-/
    66‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    67‚Üítheorem erdos_585 :
    68‚Üí    ‚àÄ n, maxEdgesNoEdgeDisjointCycles n = answer(sorry) := by
    69‚Üí  sorry
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üí/--
    72‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 585, Lower Bound [PRS95]:
    73‚Üí
    74‚ÜíThere exists a constant $c > 0$ such that for all sufficiently large $n$,
    75‚Üíthe maximum number of edges in an $n$-vertex graph with no two edge-disjoint
    76‚Üícycles on the same vertex set is at least $c \cdot n \cdot \log(\log(n))$.
    77‚Üí-/
    78‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    79‚Üítheorem erdos_585.variants.lower_bound :
    80‚Üí    ‚àÉ c : ‚Ñù, 0 < c ‚àß
    81‚Üí    ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, N‚ÇÄ ‚â§ n ‚Üí
    82‚Üí      c * (n : ‚Ñù) * Real.log (Real.log (n : ‚Ñù)) ‚â§
    83‚Üí        (maxEdgesNoEdgeDisjointCycles n : ‚Ñù) := by
    84‚Üí  sorry
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üí/--
    87‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 585, Upper Bound [CJMM24]:
    88‚Üí
    89‚ÜíThere exists a constant $C > 0$ such that for all sufficiently large $n$,
    90‚Üíthe maximum number of edges in an $n$-vertex graph with no two edge-disjoint
    91‚Üícycles on the same vertex set is at most $n \cdot (\log n)^C$.
    92‚Üí-/
    93‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    94‚Üítheorem erdos_585.variants.upper_bound :
    95‚Üí    ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, 0 < C ‚àß
    96‚Üí    ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, N‚ÇÄ ‚â§ n ‚Üí
    97‚Üí      (maxEdgesNoEdgeDisjointCycles n : ‚Ñù) ‚â§
    98‚Üí        (n : ‚Ñù) * (Real.log (n : ‚Ñù)) ^ C := by
    99‚Üí  sorry

    22‚ÜíLet $G$ be either $Q_3$ or $K_{3,3}$ or $H_5$ (the last formed by adding two vertex-disjoint chords
    23‚Üíto $C_5$). Is it true that, if $H$ has $m$ edges and no isolated vertices, then

    39‚Üí/-- $Q_3$ is the 3-dimensional hypercube graph (8 vertices, 12 edges).
    40‚ÜíVertices are 3-bit vectors. Two vertices are adjacent iff they differ in exactly one bit. -/
    41‚Üídef Q3 : SimpleGraph (Fin 3 ‚Üí Bool) where

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 149
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíThe Erd≈ës‚ÄìNe≈°et≈ôil conjecture states that the strong chromatic index of any graph with
    23‚Üímaximum degree Œî is at most 5Œî¬≤/4.
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/149](https://www.erdosproblems.com/149)
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[ErNe85] Erd≈ës, P. and Ne≈°et≈ôil, J., proposed at a seminar in Prague (1985).
    28‚Üí-/
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üínamespace Erdos149
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí/-- A set $S$ of edges of $G$ is strongly independent if for any two distinct edges
    35‚Üí$e_1, e_2 \in S$, every endpoint $u$ of $e_1$ and every endpoint $v$ of $e_2$ satisfy
    36‚Üí$u \neq v$ and $\neg G.\text{Adj}\; u\; v$. Equivalently, $S$ is an independent set in $L(G)^2$
    37‚Üí(the square of the line graph of $G$). -/
    38‚Üídef IsStronglyIndepEdgeSet {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V)
    39‚Üí    (S : Set (Sym2 V)) : Prop :=
    40‚Üí  S ‚äÜ G.edgeSet ‚àß
    41‚Üí  ‚àÄ e‚ÇÅ ‚àà S, ‚àÄ e‚ÇÇ ‚àà S, e‚ÇÅ ‚âÝ e‚ÇÇ ‚Üí
    42‚Üí    ‚àÄ u ‚àà e‚ÇÅ, ‚àÄ v ‚àà e‚ÇÇ, u ‚âÝ v ‚àß ¬¨G.Adj u v
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- The strong chromatic index $\chi'_s(G)$: the minimum number of strongly
    45‚Üíindependent edge sets needed to partition the edges of $G$. Equivalently,
    46‚Üíthis is the chromatic number of $L(G)^2$, the square of the line graph. -/
    47‚Üínoncomputable def strongChromaticIndex {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ñï :=
    48‚Üí  sInf {k : ‚Ñï | ‚àÉ (c : G.edgeSet ‚Üí Fin k),
    49‚Üí    ‚àÄ (e‚ÇÅ e‚ÇÇ : G.edgeSet), c e‚ÇÅ = c e‚ÇÇ ‚Üí e‚ÇÅ ‚âÝ e‚ÇÇ ‚Üí
    50‚Üí      ‚àÄ u ‚àà (e‚ÇÅ : Sym2 V), ‚àÄ v ‚àà (e‚ÇÇ : Sym2 V), u ‚âÝ v ‚àß ¬¨G.Adj u v}
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí/-- Erd≈ës‚ÄìNe≈°et≈ôil Strong Edge Coloring Conjecture (Erd≈ës Problem 149) [ErNe85]:
    53‚ÜíFor any finite graph $G$ with maximum degree $\Delta$,
    54‚Üí$$
    55‚Üí  \chi'_s(G) \leq \frac{5}{4} \Delta^2,
    56‚Üí$$
    57‚Üíi.e. the edge set of $G$ can be partitioned into at most $\frac{5}{4}\Delta^2$ strongly
    58‚Üíindependent edge sets. This bound is sharp: a blowup of $C_5$ with $\Delta = 2k$
    59‚Üírequires exactly $5k^2 = \frac{5}{4}\Delta^2$ strong colors. -/
    60‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    61‚Üítheorem erdos_149 :
    62‚Üí    ‚àÄ (V : Type*) [Fintype V] [DecidableEq V]
    63‚Üí      (G : SimpleGraph V) [DecidableRel G.Adj],
    64‚Üí      (strongChromaticIndex G : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ (5 / 4 : ‚Ñù) * (G.maxDegree : ‚Ñù) ^ 2 := by
    65‚Üí  sorry
    66‚Üí
    67‚Üíend Erdos149

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 666
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/666](https://www.erdosproblems.com/666)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $Q_n$ be the $n$-dimensional hypercube graph (so that $Q_n$ has $2^n$ vertices
    25‚Üíand $n \cdot 2^{n-1}$ edges). Is it true that, for every $\epsilon > 0$, if $n$ is
    26‚Üísufficiently large, every subgraph of $Q_n$ with $\geq \epsilon \cdot n \cdot 2^{n-1}$
    27‚Üímany edges contains a $C_6$?
    28‚Üí
    29‚ÜíThe answer is no: Chung [Ch92] and Brouwer, Dejter, and Thomassen [BDT93] constructed
    30‚Üían edge-partition of $Q_n$ into four subgraphs, each containing no $C_6$.
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí[Er91] Erd≈ës, P., *Some of my favourite problems in various branches of combinatorics*.
    33‚ÜíMatematiche (Catania) 47 (1992), no. 2, 231-240 (1993).
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí[Er92b] Erd≈ës, P., *Some of my old and new problems in elementary number theory and
    36‚Üígeometry*. Proceedings of the Fifth Canadian Number Theory Association Conference (1992).
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí[Er97f] Erd≈ës, P., *Some recent problems and results in graph theory*. Discrete Math.
    39‚Üí164 (1997), no. 1-3, 81-85.
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí[Ch92] Chung, F., *Subgraphs of a hypercube containing no small even cycles*. J. Graph
    42‚ÜíTheory 16 (1992), no. 3, 273-286.
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí[BDT93] Brouwer, A. E., Dejter, I. J., and Thomassen, C., *Highly symmetric subgraphs
    45‚Üíof hypercubes*. J. Algebraic Combin. 2 (1993), no. 1, 25-29.
    46‚Üí-/
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üíopen SimpleGraph Finset
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üínamespace Erdos666
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí/-- The $n$-dimensional hypercube graph $Q_n$. Vertices are functions
    53‚Üí$\operatorname{Fin} n \to \operatorname{Bool}$, and two vertices are adjacent iff they
    54‚Üídiffer in exactly one coordinate. -/
    55‚Üídef hypercubeGraph (n : ‚Ñï) : SimpleGraph (Fin n ‚Üí Bool) where
    56‚Üí  Adj u v := u ‚âÝ v ‚àß (Finset.univ.filter (fun i => u i ‚âÝ v i)).card = 1
    57‚Üí  symm u v := by
    58‚Üí    rintro ‚ü®hne, hcard‚ü©
    59‚Üí    refine ‚ü®hne.symm, ?_‚ü©
    60‚Üí    have heq : (Finset.univ.filter fun i => v i ‚âÝ u i) =
    61‚Üí               (Finset.univ.filter fun i => u i ‚âÝ v i) :=
    62‚Üí      Finset.filter_congr (fun i _ => ne_comm)
    63‚Üí    rw [heq]
    64‚Üí    exact hcard
    65‚Üí  loopless v := fun ‚ü®hne, _‚ü© => hne rfl
    66‚Üí
    67‚Üí/-- The cycle graph $C_m$ on $m$ vertices ($m \geq 3$). Vertex $i$ is adjacent to vertex
    68‚Üí$i + 1 \pmod{m}$ and vertex $i - 1 \pmod{m}$. -/
    69‚Üídef cycleGraph (m : ‚Ñï) (_ : m ‚â• 3) : SimpleGraph (Fin m) where
    70‚Üí  Adj i j := i ‚âÝ j ‚àß (j.val = (i.val + 1) % m ‚à® i.val = (j.val + 1) % m)
    71‚Üí  symm := fun _ _ ‚ü®hne, h‚ü© => ‚ü®hne.symm, h.elim Or.inr Or.inl‚ü©
    72‚Üí  loopless _ := fun ‚ü®h, _‚ü© => h rfl
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üí/--
    75‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 666 (disproved) [Er91][Er92b][Er97f]:
    76‚Üí
    77‚ÜíIs it true that, for every $\epsilon > 0$, if $n$ is sufficiently large, every subgraph of $Q_n$
    78‚Üíwith $\geq \epsilon \cdot n \cdot 2^{n-1}$ edges contains a $C_6$?
    79‚Üí
    80‚ÜíThe answer is no. Chung [Ch92] and Brouwer, Dejter, and Thomassen [BDT93] constructed
    81‚Üían edge-partition of $Q_n$ into four subgraphs, each containing no $C_6$.
    82‚Üí-/
    83‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    84‚Üítheorem erdos_666 : answer(False) ‚Üî
    85‚Üí    (‚àÄ Œµ : ‚Ñù, Œµ > 0 ‚Üí
    86‚Üí      ‚àÉ N : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• N ‚Üí
    87‚Üí      ‚àÄ H : SimpleGraph (Fin n ‚Üí Bool),

    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 622
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/622](https://www.erdosproblems.com/622)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $G$ be a regular graph with $2n$ vertices and degree $n+1$. Must $G$ have $\gg 2^{2n}$
    25‚Üísubsets that are spanned by a cycle?
    26‚Üí
    27‚ÜíA problem of Erd≈ës and Faudree [Er99]. Erd≈ës writes 'it is easy to see' that
    28‚Üíthere are at least $(1/2 + o(1))2^{2n}$ sets that are NOT on a cycle.
    29‚Üí
    30‚ÜíIf the regularity condition is replaced by minimum degree $n+1$ then the answer is
    31‚Üíno (consider $K_{n,n}$ with a spanning star in each part). Similarly this is false
    32‚Üíwith degree $n$, as $K_{n,n}$ shows.
    33‚Üí
    34‚ÜíThis has been resolved by Draganiƒá, Keevash, and M√ºyesser [DKM25], who prove the
    35‚Üíasymptotically tight result that there are at least $(1/2 + o(1))2^{2n}$ subsets
    36‚Üíwhich are spanned by a cycle.
    37‚Üí-/
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üíopen scoped Classical
    40‚Üíopen SimpleGraph Finset
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üínamespace Erdos622
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- A subset $S$ of vertices is *spanned by a cycle* in $G$ if there exists
    45‚Üí    a cycle in $G$ whose vertex set is exactly $S$. -/
    46‚Üídef IsSpannedByCycle {n : ‚Ñï} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : Prop :=
    47‚Üí  ‚àÉ v ‚àà S, ‚àÉ p : G.Walk v v, p.IsCycle ‚àß p.support.toFinset = S
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí/-- The number of subsets of vertices spanned by a cycle in a graph on $2n$ vertices. -/
    50‚Üínoncomputable def numCycleSpannedSubsets (n : ‚Ñï)
    51‚Üí    (G : SimpleGraph (Fin (2 * n))) : ‚Ñï :=
    52‚Üí  ((Finset.univ : Finset (Fin (2 * n))).powerset.filter (IsSpannedByCycle G)).card
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí/--
    55‚Üí**Erd≈ës Problem 622** [Er99]:
    56‚Üí
    57‚ÜíLet $G$ be a $(n+1)$-regular graph on $2n$ vertices. Then for any $\varepsilon > 0$ and
    58‚Üísufficiently large $n$, $G$ has at least $(1/2 - \varepsilon) \cdot 2^{2n}$ subsets that are
    59‚Üíspanned by a cycle.
    60‚Üí
    61‚ÜíProved by Draganiƒá, Keevash, and M√ºyesser [DKM25].
    62‚Üí-/
    63‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    64‚Üítheorem erdos_622 :
    65‚Üí    answer(True) ‚Üî
    66‚Üí    ‚àÄ Œµ : ‚Ñù, Œµ > 0 ‚Üí
    67‚Üí    ‚àÉ N : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• N ‚Üí
    68‚Üí    ‚àÄ G : SimpleGraph (Fin (2 * n)),
    69‚Üí      G.IsRegularOfDegree (n + 1) ‚Üí
    70‚Üí      (numCycleSpannedSubsets n G : ‚Ñù) ‚â• (1 / 2 - Œµ) * (2 : ‚Ñù) ^ (2 * n) := by
    71‚Üí  sorry
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üíend Erdos622

    47‚Üívertex-disjoint paths if there exist $m$ paths from $u$ to $v$ such that
    48‚Üíno internal vertex of one path appears as an internal vertex of another. -/

    51‚Üí  ‚àÉ (paths : Fin m ‚Üí G.Walk u v),
    52‚Üí    (‚àÄ i, (paths i).IsPath) ‚àß
    53‚Üí    (‚àÄ i j, i ‚âÝ j ‚Üí ‚àÄ w : V, w ‚âÝ u ‚Üí w ‚âÝ v ‚Üí

    63‚Üí$m \geq 6$. The edge-disjoint analogue was confirmed by Mader.

    69‚Üí    G.edgeFinset.card ‚â• 1 + n * m.choose 2 ‚Üí
    70‚Üí    ‚àÉ u v : Fin (1 + n * (m - 1)), u ‚âÝ v ‚àß

    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $G$ be a graph on $n$ vertices with chromatic number $\chi(G)$ and let $\sigma(G)$ be
    25‚Üíthe maximal $k$ such that $G$ contains a subdivision of $K_k$. Is it true that
    26‚Üí$$\chi(G) \ll \frac{n^{1/2}}{\log n} \cdot \sigma(G)?$$

    28‚ÜíHaj√≥s originally conjectured that $\chi(G) \leq \sigma(G)$, which was proved by Dirac
    29‚Üíwhen $\chi(G) = 4$. Catlin disproved Haj√≥s' conjecture for all $\chi(G) \geq 7$, and

    32‚Üí
    33‚ÜíThe answer is yes, proved by Fox, Lee, and Sudakov [FLS13].
    34‚Üí

    36‚Üíconjectures of Haj√≥s and Erd≈ës‚ÄìFajtlowicz_, Combinatorica 33 (2013), 181‚Äì197.

    45‚Üí    between every pair of branch vertices. -/
    46‚Üídef ContainsSubdivision {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V]
    47‚Üí    (G : SimpleGraph V) (k : ‚Ñï) : Prop :=
    48‚Üí  ‚àÉ (f : Fin k ‚Ü™ V)
    49‚Üí    (paths : ‚àÄ (i j : Fin k), i < j ‚Üí G.Walk (f i) (f j)),
    50‚Üí    -- Each walk is a path (no repeated vertices)
    51‚Üí    (‚àÄ i j (h : i < j), (paths i j h).IsPath) ‚àß
    52‚Üí    -- Internal vertices of different paths are disjoint
    53‚Üí    (‚àÄ i‚ÇÅ j‚ÇÅ (h‚ÇÅ : i‚ÇÅ < j‚ÇÅ) i‚ÇÇ j‚ÇÇ (h‚ÇÇ : i‚ÇÇ < j‚ÇÇ),
    54‚Üí      (i‚ÇÅ, j‚ÇÅ) ‚âÝ (i‚ÇÇ, j‚ÇÇ) ‚Üí
    55‚Üí      ‚àÄ v, v ‚àà (paths i‚ÇÅ j‚ÇÅ h‚ÇÅ).support.tail.dropLast ‚Üí
    56‚Üí           v ‚àâ (paths i‚ÇÇ j‚ÇÇ h‚ÇÇ).support.tail.dropLast) ‚àß
    57‚Üí    -- Internal vertices are disjoint from branch vertices
    58‚Üí    (‚àÄ i j (h : i < j) v,
    59‚Üí      v ‚àà (paths i j h).support.tail.dropLast ‚Üí
    60‚Üí      ‚àÄ m : Fin k, v ‚âÝ f m)
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí/-- $\sigma(G)$ is the maximum $k$ such that $G$ contains a subdivision of $K_k$.
    63‚Üí    For a graph on a finite vertex type, this is always finite. -/
    64‚Üínoncomputable def subdivisionNumber {V : Type*} [Fintype V] [DecidableEq V]
    65‚Üí    (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ñï :=
    66‚Üí  sSup {k : ‚Ñï | ContainsSubdivision G k}
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üí/--
    69‚Üí**Erd≈ës Problem 717** (PROVED, Fox‚ÄìLee‚ÄìSudakov [FLS13]):
    70‚Üí
    71‚ÜíFor every graph $G$ on $n$ vertices,
    72‚Üí$$\chi(G) \leq C \cdot \frac{\sqrt{n}}{\log n} \cdot \sigma(G)$$
    73‚Üífor some absolute constant $C > 0$.
    74‚Üí-/
    75‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    76‚Üítheorem erdos_717 : answer(True) ‚Üî
    77‚Üí    ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, C > 0 ‚àß
    78‚Üí      ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï), 2 ‚â§ n ‚Üí
    79‚Üí        ‚àÄ (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)),
